lessons.title: The Opposition
lessons.description: Learn to use the opposition to win King and Pawn endgames.

steps.instruction: You have King and Pawn vs King. The key is OPPOSITION - Kings facing each other. First, advance with Kd5 to outflank!

feedback.correct: Excellent! Your King advances to support the pawn and outflank Black.
feedback.incorrect: Try Kd5 - outflank the Black King to gain control.

steps.instruction: Black mirrors you. Now push e6 to gain space!

feedback.correct: The pawn advances! Black must react.
feedback.incorrect: Push e6 - advance the pawn while your King supports it.

steps.instruction: Black retreats. Now play Ke5 to take the OPPOSITION!

feedback.correct: YES! You have the opposition! Kings face each other and Black must give way!
feedback.incorrect: Play Ke5 - this gives you the opposition (Kings facing each other, opponent to move).

steps.instruction: Black had to retreat! Now Kd6 to maintain opposition and escort the pawn!

feedback.correct: Perfect! Opposition maintained. The pawn will promote!
feedback.incorrect: Kd6 keeps the opposition and prepares e7.

steps.instruction: Push the pawn! Play e7 to reach the 7th rank!

feedback.correct: The pawn is on the 7th rank! Promotion is inevitable.
feedback.incorrect: Push e7 - one more step to queening!

steps.instruction: Black tries to block. Play Kd7 to protect the pawn and ensure promotion!

feedback.correct: The King escorts the pawn. Promotion next move!
feedback.incorrect: Kd7 protects the pawn - it will promote!

lessons.successMessage: Excellent! You've learned the key concept of Opposition in King and Pawn endings!
hints: The opposition means Kings face each other with odd number of squares between
The side NOT to move has the opposition
Use 'shouldering' to push the enemy King away

lessons.title: Lucena Position - Building the Bridge
lessons.description: Master the famous Lucena winning technique.

steps.instruction: This is the LUCENA position. Your Rook needs to 'build a bridge' to shield the King from checks. First, play Rg1!

feedback.correct: The Rook goes to the g-file, preparing the bridge!
feedback.incorrect: Play Rg1 - we're setting up the famous 'bridge' technique.

steps.instruction: Black's King moves aside. Play Kc7 to step away from the pawn.

feedback.correct: Good! Making room for the pawn to promote.
feedback.incorrect: Try Kc7 - the King needs to step aside.

steps.instruction: Now the key move! Play Rg4 - the bridge is being built on the 4th rank!

feedback.correct: THIS is the bridge! The Rook on the 4th rank will block checks!
feedback.incorrect: Rg4 is the key move - placing the Rook on the 4th rank builds the bridge.

lessons.successMessage: You've learned the Lucena 'Building a Bridge' technique! This wins every Rook endgame with pawn on 7th!
hints: The Rook goes to the 4th rank to block future checks
The bridge technique shields the King from Rook checks

lessons.title: Square of the Pawn
lessons.description: Learn if the King can catch the pawn!

steps.instruction: Can Black's King catch your pawn? Calculate the 'Square of the Pawn'. Is the King inside? Push a5!

feedback.correct: The Square of the Pawn is a4-a8-e8-e4. Black's King on g2 is OUTSIDE! The pawn promotes!
feedback.incorrect: Push a5 - the Black King can't catch your pawn!

feedback.correct: PROMOTION! You now have a Queen!
feedback.incorrect: a8=Q promotes the pawn to a Queen!

lessons.successMessage: The 'Square of the Pawn' rule helps you instantly know if the King can catch a pawn!
hints: Draw a diagonal from the pawn to the promotion square
If the King is inside that square, it catches the pawn
